# zidl â€” Java Backend Reference

Generated by `zidl -b java`. Produces `.java` source files with inline CDR
serialization â€” no companion runtime package is required. Follows the IDL4-to-Java
mapping (formal/21-08-01 v1.0).

---

## Generated Files

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `Types.java` | All types from the IDL (default single-file mode) |

With `--split-files`, one `.java` file is produced per top-level named type, named
after the type (e.g. `MyStruct.java`, `MyEnum.java`).

---

## Type Mapping

| IDL type | Java type |
|---|---|
| `boolean` | `boolean` |
| `octet` | `byte` |
| `char` | `char` |
| `wchar` | `char` |
| `short` / `int16` | `short` |
| `unsigned short` / `uint16` | `short` |
| `long` / `int32` | `int` |
| `unsigned long` / `uint32` | `int` |
| `long long` / `int64` | `long` |
| `unsigned long long` / `uint64` | `long` |
| `float` | `float` |
| `double` | `double` |
| `long double` | `double` |
| `fixed<D,S>` | literal value |
| `string` / `wstring` | `String` |
| `sequence<T>` | `java.util.ArrayList<T>` |
| `T[N]` | `T[]` |
| `map<K,V>` | `java.util.Map<K, V>` |
| `@optional T` | nullable field (`T` reference, default `null`) |
| `enum` | Java `enum` with `int value` field and `fromValue(int)` factory |
| `bitmask` | Java `enum` with `int value` field |
| `bitset` | Java `class` with integer storage field |
| `struct` | Java `class` (public fields, default constructor, copy constructor) |
| `union` | Java `class` with discriminant field + typed case methods |
| IDL module | Java package (with `--java-package` prefix prepended) |

---

## Generated File Structure

```java
package com.example;   // from --java-package com.example

// Inline CDR helpers â€” generated once per output file
// (ZidlCdrWriter, ZidlCdrReader inner classes)

public class MyStruct {
    public int x = 0;
    public String name = "";

    public MyStruct() {}

    public MyStruct(MyStruct other) { ... }   // copy constructor

    public static void serialize(java.nio.ByteBuffer buf, MyStruct v) { ... }
    public static MyStruct deserialize(java.nio.ByteBuffer buf) { ... }

    // For keyed types:
    public static void serializeKey(java.nio.ByteBuffer buf, MyStruct v) { ... }
    public static MyStruct deserializeKey(java.nio.ByteBuffer buf, int cdrBase) { ... }
    public byte[] computeKeyHash() { ... }
}

public enum MyEnum {
    VALUE_A(0),
    VALUE_B(1);

    public final int value;
    MyEnum(int v) { this.value = v; }

    public static MyEnum fromValue(int v) { ... }
}
```

---

## CDR Serialize/Deserialize Signatures

```java
/* Serialize v into buf at the current position. */
public static void serialize(java.nio.ByteBuffer buf, Foo v);

/* Deserialize from buf at the current position. */
public static Foo deserialize(java.nio.ByteBuffer buf);
```

CDR helpers (`ZidlCdrWriter`, `ZidlCdrReader`) are generated inline in the output file;
there is no external runtime jar or dependency.

---

## Key Serialization

For structs with `@key` members:

```java
/* Serialize key fields only as canonical PLAIN_CDR2 big-endian */
public static void serializeKey(java.nio.ByteBuffer buf, Foo v);

/* Deserialize key fields from a full CDR stream; non-key fields zeroed */
public static Foo deserializeKey(java.nio.ByteBuffer buf, int cdrBase);

/* Deserialize key fields into an existing instance */
public static void deserializeKeyInto(Foo out, java.nio.ByteBuffer buf, int cdrBase);

/* Compute RTPS key hash: key â‰¤ 16 bytes â†’ zero-pad; > 16 bytes â†’ MD5 */
public byte[] computeKeyHash();
```

MD5 uses `java.security.MessageDigest.getInstance("MD5")` â€” no external dependency.

---

## `--generate-interfaces` and JNI

When `--generate-interfaces` is set, each IDL `interface` declaration produces a
`*Impl.java` class that loads a native JNI library:

```java
public class FooImpl {
    static {
        System.loadLibrary("my_dds_lib");   // from --java-jni-library my_dds_lib
    }
    // native method declarations
}
```

Pass the library name with `--java-jni-library <name>`.

---

## Backend-Specific Options

| Option | Effect |
|---|---|
| `--java-package <pkg>` | Package prefix prepended to IDL module path (e.g. `com.example`) |
| `--split-files` | One `.java` file per top-level type |
| `--java-jni-library <name>` | `System.loadLibrary()` name for `--generate-interfaces` JNI bridge |
| `--no-typesupport` | Suppress CDR serialize/deserialize output |

---

## Integration Test

The Java integration test in `test/integration/java/Test.java` compiles against the
committed golden `test/golden/java/Types.java` using `javac` and runs with `java`.
Zig's build system automatically skips the Java tests if `javac`/`java` is not on `PATH`.

Run directly:

```sh
zig build integration-test   # runs all integration tests including Java if javac found
```

---

## Known Limitations

| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| `bitset` CDR serialization | Emits `// TODO: bitset` â€” no standard Java CDR mapping defined |
| `any` / `object` / `value_base` member access | Emits `// TODO: any/object` comment |
